title: Named exports and imports
instruction:
TypeScript uses ES module syntax. Export functions and types with `export`, import them with `import { ... } from`.

Run this.
successMessage: In a real project you'd split these into separate files and use `export`/`import`. TypeScript checks that imported names actually exist in the source file.

title: Type aliases as module contracts
instruction:
Exported type aliases and interfaces define the contract between modules. They're erased at runtime but checked at compile time.

Run this.
successMessage: Generic interfaces like `ApiResponse<T>` are a common pattern for typed API wrappers. They enforce consistent response shapes across your entire codebase.
